HomePage.title: The <headline>faster</headline> age of plurality bots
HomePage.desc:
PluralBuddy is the next generation plurality bot for Discord – built on

principals of speed, accessibility & data convenience.
HomePage.oss_about: PluralBuddy is open-source – and will stay that way. All code running on your server or system is overseen by other individuals.
HomePage.optimized_desc: At our forefront – PluralBuddy is built for systems and designed by systems.
HomePage.instant_desc: Auto-proxy settings allow you to proxy whenever you like – whether that's when you use a proxy tag, or whenever you send a message, PluralBuddy is ready when you are.
HomePage.identity_desc: Send a webhook or change your nickname – its up to you.<sup>2</sup> Changing your nickname can be formatted however you please.
HomePage.quick_desc: Proxy quickly, as fast as 600ms<sup>1</sup>! PluralBuddy was built from the ground up on being the bot that doesn't mess around with proxying.
HomePage.components_desc: With the power of Components v2, most features on PluralBuddy can be done using plain old buttons as opposed to commands. However, you can pick and choice which one you'd like to use!
HomePage.administrate_desc: PluralBuddy has numerous administrator related features – designed to keep a balance between admin and user control.
HomePage.change_bot_title: Disable or enable different features of the bot
HomePage.change_bot_desc: PluralBuddy is split into feature flags, dynamically changing the features allowed on PluralBuddy based on your server's guidelines or needs
HomePage.alter_containers_desc: Add Discord containers to messages to show members that your staff members are chatting – even if they are currently proxying.
HomePage.permissions_title: Permissions work just how normal messages work.
HomePage.permissions_desc: PluralBuddy checks the permissions of users when they send a message to ensure they are sending messages and only using specific features when they are permitted by their roles.
HomePage.get_started_desc: PluralBuddy was built with love and pride as an alternative to the plurality bots you see everywhere. Migration from other bots like PluralKit or TupperBox can be done simply by using the <mono>pb;setup</mono> command when adding it to a server and selecting the Import option when going through the setup wizard.
HomePage.sup_1: After PluralBuddy gains information about a message from the Discord Gateway, it usually takes under 600ms in optimal conditions to proxy a message.
HomePage.sup_2: Webhook proxy modes or nickname proxy modes can be enforced by a server administrator, made by decisions not inherently approved by PluralBuddy or its developers.

FooterComponent.lgbt_lives_matter: 🏳️‍⚧️🏳️‍🌈 LGBTQIA+ lives matter. PluralBuddy and its team support all individuals regardless of their identity.

ConsentPage.title: {client_name} is attempting to access data from your system

scopes.profile: Access to your profile data – required.
scopes.openid: Access to your Discord user ID
scopes.offline_access: Access to refresh access to this service
scopes.tags:alters: Access to assign tags to alters
scopes.system:admin: Access to read and write to systems AND alters.

ExpressModal.page0_desc: PluralBuddy associates an alter that already exists with the new Express application you create. Choose an alter you'd like to represent your new Express application.
ExpressModal.page1_desc: Due to how PluralBuddy Express works, you need to create an application for each alter that you want to assign with Express.
ExpressModal.stepper1_desc: You must go to the Discord Developer Portal to create an application for PluralBuddy.
ExpressModal.stepper2_desc: Hit the Bot Tab -> Reset token. Don't worry, this token is encrypted in our database.
ExpressModal.error_creating:
Error while creating express application.

Error: {message}

ExpressList.desc: Create user-proxies with PluralBuddy Express – assign your own alters to Discord applications and use native Discord slash commands to proxy as an alter. Works in direct messages, or any server with Use External Apps granted.

ExpressPage.empty_desc: This alter does exist in your system, however it doesn't have an associated PluralBuddy Express Application.
ExpressPage.add_app_description: Authorize this application and use the /{alterName} command to get started with Express!
ExpressPage.profile_desc: Your application profile is shown on the left (or above on mobile). PluralBuddy can sync your profile between what is shown in your system and the user data on your Express Application, however it is not automatic.
ExpressPage.delete_desc:
By deleting this Express application, it will no longer be associated with a native Discord application. This will not delete the alter, just the association to a native Discord application. Using this applications commands after deletion will result in an error from Discord. This action cannot be undone.



To delete this object, type {alter} in the input below to permanently:
